input_row.title: Ensure 'FTP state' is set to 'FTPS only' or 'Disabled'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, set FTP state to FTPS only or Disabled. 5. Click Save. 6. Click Continue. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each function app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each function app requiring remediation, run the following command to set FTPS state to FtpsOnly or Disabled: az functionapp config set --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name> --ftps-state <FtpsOnly|Disabled> Default Value: By default, FTP state is set to FTPS only. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/deploy-ftp 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-security 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/functions-how-to-use- azure-function-app-settings#ftps-deployment-settings 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- data-protection#dp-4-encrypt-sensitive-information-in-transit 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- posture-vulnerability-management#pv-7-rapidly-and-automatically-remediate- software-vulnerabilities 7. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.functions/get- azfunctionapp CIS Controls:
input_row.reference: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/deploy-ftp 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-security 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/functions-how-to-use- azure-function-app-settings#ftps-deployment-settings 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- data-protection#dp-4-encrypt-sensitive-information-in-transit 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- posture-vulnerability-management#pv-7-rapidly-and-automatically-remediate- software-vulnerabilities 7. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.functions/get- azfunctionapp CIS Controls: Controls Version Control IG 1 IG 2 IG 3 v8 3.10 Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH). ● ● v7 14.4 Encrypt All Sensitive Information in Transit Encrypt all sensitive information in transit.  ● ●

first_opinion.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control specifies the use of Azure CLI commands for verifying and remediating the FTP state, indicating that the property can be queried and modified through Azure APIs.
- The properties of Azure Function Apps, including the FTP state, are accessible through Azure Resource Manager APIs and Azure CLI, satisfying the automated criteria.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_functionapp_ftp_state_ftps_only_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The automated program would check the FTP state configuration for all Function Apps within an Azure subscription.
- Azure CLI command to use: 
  ```bash
  az functionapp config show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name>
  ```
- Specific resources to enumerate includes all Function Apps in each resource group of the subscription.
- Check the setting of `ftpsState` property:
  - Expected Pass criteria: `ftpsState` should be either `FtpsOnly` or `Disabled`.
  - Expected Fail criteria: `ftpsState` should be `AllAllowed` or any setting that does not comply with the control.
- Example API endpoint: 
  - To get the Function App configuration, you might use: 
    - `GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/{siteName}/config/web?api-version=2020-12-01` 
- The automated check would return either Pass or Fail based on the `ftpsState` value retrieved.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- Keep as-is, with a suggestion to enhance the automation script with error handling and remediation capabilities.
- Consider documenting the necessary permissions and roles required to perform these operations to ensure the automation can be executed without permission issues.

final_decision.response:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that the audit approach should be automated, as the FTP state of Azure Function Apps can be checked and modified using Azure CLI and Azure Resource Manager APIs. The properties required for compliance verification, such as `ftpsState`, are accessible through these tools, making automation feasible and efficient.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
azure_functionapp_ftp_state_ftps_only_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **Azure Resource/Service:** Function Apps
- **API/CLI Method:** `az functionapp config show`
- **Properties to Check:** `ftpsState`
- **Pass Criteria:** `ftpsState` should be either `FtpsOnly` or `Disabled`
- **Fail Criteria:** Any other value for `ftpsState` indicates non-compliance

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported the automated approach, and the technical details provided were accurate and aligned with Azure capabilities.
